content.intro: By law, all tenant deposits must be protected in a government-approved scheme within 30 days of receipt. PropertyGoose integrates with TDS (Tenancy Deposit Scheme) to make this process simple.

steps.title: Find the tenancy
steps.content: Go to Tenancies and locate the tenancy with an unprotected deposit. Look for the deposit protection indicator showing 'Unprotected'.

steps.title: Click 'Protect Deposit'
steps.content: Click the 'Protect Deposit' button to open the TDS registration modal.

steps.title: Verify deposit details
steps.content: Review the deposit amount and tenant details. Ensure the information is correct as it will be submitted to TDS.

steps.title: Confirm protection
steps.content: Click 'Register with TDS' to submit the deposit for protection. This connects to TDS and registers the deposit.

steps.title: Issue Section 48 notice
steps.content: After protection, you must provide tenants with the prescribed information. Use the Section 48 notice generator to create and send this document.

sections.title: 30-Day Deadline
sections.content: You must protect the deposit within 30 days of receiving it. Failure to do so can result in penalties of up to 3x the deposit amount and prevent you from serving a Section 21 notice.

sections.title: Checking Protection Status
sections.content: The tenancy row shows the deposit protection status. Green indicates protected, while amber or red indicates unprotected or issues. The Dashboard also shows a count of unprotected deposits in the stats cards.

sections.title: TDS Integration Required
sections.content: To use automatic deposit protection, you need to configure your TDS credentials in Settings > Integrations. Contact your TDS provider for API access.
